title: galactia glaucophylla
intro: Galactia glaucophylla, also known as the small white milkpea, is a perennial vine native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. Consuming Galactia glaucophylla has been associated with various health benefits, including the relief of menstrual cramps, the reduction of fever, and the improvement of digestive issues. Galactia glaucophylla can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping its dried leaves or flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting its active compounds with a solvent.
